texto: Whatever we know as children, this is the world, eaten whole and without question.

texto: Once she had thought that she might discover some key to her mother if only she could get her likeness right, but she has since learned that the mysteries of another person only deepen, the longer one looks.

texto: She is leaving him, not all at once, which would be painful enough, but in a wrenching succession of separations. One moment she is here, and then she is gone again, and each journey takes her a little farther from his reach. He cannot follow her, and he wonders where she goes when she leaves.
explicacao: Citação de Debra Dean sobre limites e escolhas conscientes. “She is leaving him, not all at once, which would be painful enough, but in a wrenching succession…” articula sucesso, aging, alzheimer-s em registro de sucesso, com relevância para contextos como viagem e alzheimer-s.

texto: Before you either turn away in disgust or wink knowingly at one another, you should know that the artist insists that this is a picture about love. Filial love. The old man has been condemned by the Roman senate to die of hunger, and his daughter has come to his prison cell and offered her breast to feed him. This has nothing to do with with the decorous love or amorous passions one is more accustomed to seeing in a painting. It is raw and wretched and demeaning. In the end, we are physical bodies and every abstract notion about love sinks beneath this fact.
explicacao: Orientação direta de Debra Dean sobre vínculos afetivos e cuidado mútuo. A afirmação “Before you either turn away in disgust or wink knowingly at one another, you should know that the…” funciona como lembrete prático ligado a familia, body, daughter, útil em momentos de termino e daughter.
